metrics.visibleTextStart: Skip to content CreditUnionAI News Artificial Intelligence News and Analysis for Credit Unions Coverage of artificial intelligence regulation, fraud, operations, vendors, and strategy for credit unions. Get Weekly Briefing Read Latest News LATEST AI NEWSROOM ALERT Member Service Alert Jul 22, 2026 View all Alerts OpenAI Launches Presence as BBVA Explores Banking Voice Support OpenAI launched Presence on July 22 through a limited general-availability program for eligible enterprise customers. The product supports voice and chat agents that can use approved company systems, take permitted actions and escalate to people; BBVA Mexico is a design partner exploring AI-powered voice support for everyday banking needs. What this means for credit unions: Credit unions evaluating voice agents should require strong caller verification that does not rely on voice alone, least-privilege system access, explicit approval boundaries, complete action logs and reliable staff handoffs. Pilots should also test accuracy, accessibility, complaint handling, data retention and change controls; BBVA's exploration is not evidence of production performance or credit-union readiness. Read the Alert → Primary

h1.text: Bank of America Puts Generative AI Beside 18,000 Service Employees

headings.text: Bank of America Puts Generative AI Beside 18,000 Service Employees
The design choice matters more than the headline
Controls credit unions should test first
A useful benchmark, not a transferable result
Related coverage
Turn this story into a weekly operating advantage.

metrics.visibleTextStart: Skip to content Alerts News Insights Intelligence Episodes Subscribe LinkedIn ← Back to News REPORTING Bank of America Puts Generative AI Beside 18,000 Service Employees July 22, 2026 By CreditUnionAI News Staff Member ServiceGenerative AIContact CentersHuman Oversight Bank of America has added generative AI to EricaAssist, an employee-facing service tool used by more than 18,000 representatives. The deployment gives credit unions a concrete operating model to examine: use AI to assemble guidance during a conversation while keeping the employee responsible for the interaction. Bank of America said Tuesday that it has expanded EricaAssist with generative AI that supplies contextual guidance to customer-service employees during live conversations. The bank says the tool can summarize why a client is calling, gather relevant information and recommend next steps without taking the conversation away from the employee. The July 21 Bank of America announcement says more than 18,000 service representatives use EricaAssist. It reports that guidance arrives in under three seconds and that the tool has reduced average call time by nearly one minute. Those performance figures are the bank’s ow

metrics.visibleTextStart: Skip to content ← Back to News REPORTING Bank of America Puts Generative AI Beside 18,000 Service Employees July 22, 2026 By CreditUnionAI News Staff Member ServiceGenerative AIContact CentersHuman Oversight Bank of America has added generative AI to EricaAssist, an employee-facing service tool used by more than 18,000 representatives. The deployment gives credit unions a concrete operating model to examine: use AI to assemble guidance during a conversation while keeping the employee responsible for the interaction. Bank of America said Tuesday that it has expanded EricaAssist with generative AI that supplies contextual guidance to customer-service employees during live conversations. The bank says the tool can summarize why a client is calling, gather relevant information and recommend next steps without taking the conversation away from the employee. The July 21 Bank of America announcement says more than 18,000 service representatives use EricaAssist. It reports that guidance arrives in under three seconds and that the tool has reduced average call time by nearly one minute. Those performance figures are the bank’s own and have not been independently verified. Reuters independen

metrics.visibleTextStart: Skip to content Alerts News Insights Intelligence Episodes Subscribe LinkedIn EVIDENCE LAYER Credit Union AI Intelligence Evidence-backed vendor and regulatory intelligence for credit unions, connected to current reporting and practical analysis. Track what vendors are changing, what regulators are signaling and how each development connects to credit-union functions. Records preserve primary sources and evidence labels; inclusion is not endorsement. MARKET INTELLIGENCE AI Vendor Tracker Search normalized vendor capabilities and dated signals across core, digital banking, payments, fraud, lending and enterprise technology. Explore vendors → POLICY INTELLIGENCE Regulatory Watch Follow primary-source guidance, hearings, resources and supervisory signals, mapped to affected functions and technologies. Open Regulatory Watch → How the intelligence is built CreditUnionAI News adds a structured record only when a development has an attributable source and a specific credit-union implication. Vendor claims remain clearly labeled until corroborated. Regulatory summaries link to the issuing authority and are not legal advice.
